extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series starts with a moderate value (around 0.4–0.6) and does not contain a very sharp initial spike above ~0.7 in the first 5–8 time steps.  
R2: After the start, the early segment (up to about time 15) shows one or more drops clearly below 0.3 and then recovers.  
R3: In the middle segment (roughly time 20–45) the values fluctuate around mid‑range (0.3–0.6) without a long, smooth upward ramp from low to high.  
R4: The series ends (last 10–15 points) in the mid‑range (about 0.35–0.6), without staying near the minimum (≤0.2).
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series begins with a steep descent from a relatively high initial value (around 0.6–0.8) down to a clear low (≈0.15–0.25) within the first 10–12 time steps.  
R2: After this early trough, the signal shows a fairly smooth, sustained rise from low values (~0.2) to mid/high values (~0.45–0.6) around time 20.  
R3: In the middle of the series (about time 25–45) there is at least one pronounced peak above ~0.7 followed by a deep drop close to the minimum (≈0.1–0.2).  
R4: The final segment (last 10–15 points) rises again from a low or mid value to stay around mid‑range (~0.4–0.6), forming a late upward recovery.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series starts around a medium value (≈0.4–0.6) or quickly rises to it within the first few time steps, without a strong initial downward trend.
R2: Between time 5 and 15 there is at least one relatively sharp peak where the value exceeds about 0.6–0.7.
R3: In the middle segment (roughly time 15–45) the values stay mostly in the mid-range (≈0.3–0.6) with only moderate fluctuations, and without a long, smooth monotonic decrease.
R4: In the last third (after ≈time 45) there is at most a short, shallow low; the values either recover to mid-range or remain around 0.3–0.6 without a deep, prolonged dip near 0.1.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series begins high (≈0.6–0.8) and then shows a strong, relatively smooth downward trend into a clear low (≈0.1–0.2) within the first 10–15 time steps.
R2: After this early low, the series climbs back to mid or high values (≈0.4–0.7) in the central part (≈time 15–40), often forming a broad plateau or gentle hump.
R3: Around the middle-to-late region (≈time 35–55) there is a pronounced deep dip (value near 0.1) that is sharper and lower than its immediate surroundings.
R4: Following this mid-late dip, the series ends with a recovery to mid-range values (≈0.3–0.6), without a large final peak above about 0.7.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series starts with a medium value (around 0.4–0.6) and does not have a very sharp, tall spike in the first 10 time steps.  
R2: In the first 15 time steps, the minimum value does not go extremely low (it rarely drops below ~0.1).  
R3: After time step 15, the series oscillates around the mid-range (roughly 0.3–0.6) without a long, steady low plateau.  
R4: The overall pattern looks “bumpy” throughout, with several medium peaks and valleys but no very tall, isolated peak near the beginning.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: In the first 5–10 time steps, the series forms a tall peak (reaching around 0.7–0.8) followed by a steep decline.  
R2: Soon after this initial peak (within about the first 15 time steps), the series drops to a clearly low region (near or below ~0.15) before rising again.  
R3: Between time steps 15 and 45, the series mostly stays in the mid-range (roughly 0.3–0.6), without many very low values.  
R4: Overall, the most prominent, sharp peak in the whole series appears very early (near the start), and later peaks are generally smaller or similar in height.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series starts around a medium value (≈0.4–0.6) rather than very high (≥0.7).  
R2: Within the first 10–15 time steps, the series shows at least one clear rise and one clear drop (an early “zig‑zag”) instead of a single smooth fall.  
R3: In the middle part (≈t=20–45), values remain mostly in a medium band (≈0.3–0.6) without a long, flat high plateau above 0.6.  
R4: The final segment (≈t>55) stays in the medium range (≈0.3–0.6) and does not recover from a deep valley immediately before it.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts high (first few points ≥0.7) and then drops steeply into low values (≤0.3) within the first 10–15 time steps.  
R2: After this early drop, the series climbs into a medium band (≈0.4–0.6) and forms a visible plateau or gentle undulation there (≈t=20–35).  
R3: A tall peak (≥0.7) appears in the mid‑to‑late region (≈t=35–45), clearly above the surrounding medium values.  
R4: Near the end (≈t>55), the series sits again in a medium band (≈0.4–0.6) after having visited at least one deep valley (≤0.2) shortly before.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series starts around the mid‑range (≈0.4–0.6) rather than very high (>0.7).
R2: Within the first 10 time steps there is at most one sharp peak above 0.8.
R3: Around time 15–30 the series stays mostly in a moderate band (≈0.3–0.6) without a very deep dip below 0.15.
R4: There is no long, steep monotonic drop from a very high value (>0.7) down to near 0.1 at the beginning.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts at a high value (≈0.7–0.8).
R2: Within the first 10 time steps there is a strong downward trend from that high start to a pronounced minimum near 0.1–0.2.
R3: After this early deep minimum, the series recovers to moderate or high values (≈0.4–0.7) around time 15–30.
R4: A second pronounced peak (>0.7) occurs roughly in the middle region (≈time 30–45) before later fluctuations.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series starts with a moderate value (around 0.4–0.6) and does not contain a tall, narrow spike right at the beginning (no value above about 0.8 within the first 5 time steps).  
R2: Between early and mid time (roughly indices 10–30) the values stay mostly in the mid-range (0.3–0.6) with only shallow local minima (they do not drop and stay near 0.1).  
R3: After the mid-point (around indices 30–50) there is at least one pronounced upward excursion (a local peak above about 0.6), but this peak is not immediately preceded by a very deep valley below about 0.1.  
R4: In the last part (indices 50–70) the signal returns to a mid-range band (around 0.3–0.6) without an extremely sharp recovery from a value near 0.0.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series begins with a relatively high value (around 0.6–0.8) followed by a steep drop within the first 10 time steps to a low value (around 0.1–0.2).  
R2: After this early drop, in the region roughly 10–30, the signal climbs from low values (≈0.1–0.2) to mid values (≈0.4–0.6), forming a clear rising segment.  
R3: In the middle section (around 30–45) there is a strong peak (value above about 0.7) that is part of a tall, narrow “spike-like” ascent from the surrounding mid-range.  
R4: In the later section (around 45–60) there is at least one deep dip to near 0.0–0.1 followed by a sharp rebound to a higher value (above about 0.5).

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: In the first 5–10% of time, the value does not start at a high level and drop sharply; instead it stays moderate (around 0.4–0.6) or fluctuates mildly.  
R2: In the segment from about 10% to 30% of time, there is at most one local minimum below 0.2, and values quickly recover above 0.3.  
R3: In the middle segment (about 30%–50% of time), there is at least one pronounced peak above 0.7 followed by a sharp drop toward ~0.2.  
R4: In the last 20% of time, the signal stays in a medium band (roughly 0.3–0.6) with only small fluctuations, without clear strong upward or downward trends.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: In the first 5–10% of time, the signal starts high (around 0.6–0.8) and then drops steeply to near or below 0.2.  
R2: After this first drop, in the interval up to about 30% of time, the signal shows a clear “valley then rise”: a low region near 0.1–0.2 followed by a smooth increase back to at least ~0.4–0.5.  
R3: In the middle region (about 30%–50% of time), the signal again forms a tall peak above ~0.7 that comes from a moderate level (~0.4–0.5) and falls back down to near or below 0.2.  
R4: In the last 20% of time, the signal rises from that low and remains mostly increasing or high (around 0.4–0.6), rather than staying flat in the midrange.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: The series starts with a moderate value (around the mid-range) and does not show a very steep initial drop within the first 10 time steps.  
R2: Between time steps 10 and 30, the values stay mostly in the middle band (roughly 0.3–0.6) without a prolonged flat low segment near the minimum.  
R3: Around time steps 35–45, there is a pronounced local peak (one of the higher points of the whole series) followed by a clear drop.  
R4: In the last third (time > 45), the series rises again from a low/mid value to end in the mid or upper-mid range, without remaining near the minimum for long.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: The series starts relatively high (near the maximum of the scale) and shows a sharp, steep drop within the first 10 time steps down toward low values.  
R2: After this initial drop (roughly time 10–25), there is a long low-to-mid plateau or gentle rise from low values up to mid-range, without large peaks.  
R3: Around time steps 30–45, the series exhibits a sustained tall peak (one of the highest points of the sequence) rising from the mid-range and then dropping sharply.  
R4: In the last third (time > 45), the series includes at least one deep dip to near-minimum values followed by a partial recovery to mid-range, but it does not return to the very high levels seen at the beginning.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: In the first 15% of the time series, there is at least one sharp upward spike followed by a fast drop, so that a local maximum above 0.7 is quickly followed by a local minimum below 0.3.  
R2: After time 15%, there is a mid‑level plateau region (values mostly between 0.3 and 0.6) with several small oscillations but without long stretches near the top (above 0.7) or bottom (below 0.2).  
R3: In the middle–late part (around 40–55% of the series), there is at least one deep valley (value below 0.2) preceded or followed closely by a high peak above 0.6 (strong peak–valley contrast).  
R4: In the final 20% of the series, values return to mid‑range (roughly 0.3–0.6) without another very high early‑like spike.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: In the first 15% of the time series, values start high and remain in a strong downward trend, with an early maximum above 0.7 followed by a sustained decrease toward about 0.2 or lower.  
R2: The early strong downward segment is smoother than in Class 0: it lacks an immediate sharp peak‑then‑drop pattern and instead shows a more monotonic fall.  
R3: After this early fall, the series climbs gradually to mid‑high levels (0.4–0.7) through one broad hump, rather than many small jagged oscillations.  
R4: In the last 20% of the series, values stay mostly in the mid‑range (about 0.3–0.6) with modest fluctuations, without a pronounced new global maximum or minimum.

extracted_rules.class_0:
R1: In the first 10–15% of time, the series has a moderate peak but does not exceed a very high value (stays roughly below 0.9).
R2: In the region between about 20–40% of time, the series is relatively flat or gently undulating around a mid-level (roughly 0.4–0.6) without very sharp spikes.
R3: In the region around 40–55% of time, at least one sharp deep dip occurs, where the value falls close to the minimum range (around 0.1–0.2) from a higher level.
R4: In the final 20–30% of time, the series recovers from that dip to a mid-level plateau or gentle oscillation (around 0.4–0.6), without large peaks above about 0.7.
extracted_rules.class_1:
R1: At the very beginning (first 5–10% of time), the series starts already high and quickly reaches a strong peak (around 0.7–0.8), clearly higher than most later values.
R2: Immediately after this initial peak, the series drops steeply to a low valley (around 0.1–0.2) within the next 10–15% of time.
R3: After this early valley, in the middle segment (about 20–45% of time), the values climb back to and hover around mid-to-high levels (roughly 0.5–0.7) with noticeable peaks.
R4: In the later segment (about 45–60% of time), there is another pronounced drop to near-minimum values (around 0.1–0.2), followed by a recovery to mid levels (around 0.4–0.6) toward the end.
